name: Chiu Chow Lemon Chicken
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------------------SAUCE-----------------------------------
2 tablespoons (or 1/4 cup) Grated lemon peel or finely minced lemon peel
1/2 cup Lemon juice
6 tablespoons Sugar
1/4 cup Chicken broth
2 tablespoons Light soy sauce
1/4 teaspoon Salt
---------------------------------TO FINISH---------------------------------
2 tablespoons Cornstarch mixed with an equal amount of cold water
1/4 cup Cooking oil
Salt and pepper to taste
1 cup All-purpose flour
4 tablespoons Unsalted butter
ADVANCE PREPARATION:
Dip each chicken breast in water, then gently flatten with a meat pounder or rolling pin until the breast increases in size by about one-third. Set aside.
Toast almonds in a 325°F oven until light golden, then set aside.
Mince chives and set aside.
Combine garlic and ginger and set aside.
LAST-MINUTE COOKING:
In a small bowl combine sauce ingredients and mix well.
Place a small noncorrosive skillet or saucepan over medium heat. Add 1 tablespoon cooking oil and then garlic and ginger. Saute briefly, then add the sauce. Bring to a low boil and reduce heat to simmer.
Sprinkle chicken on both sides with salt and pepper. Dust with flour, shaking off any excess. Place a 12-inch skillet over high heat. When hot, add butter and remaining 3 tablespoons oil. When butter bubbles, pan-fry 3 or 4 chicken pieces at a time. Cook for about 1 minute on each side until the meat turns color and feels firm to touch (do not overcook). Transfer chicken to a heated platter or dinner plates and keep warm in the oven while cooking remaining chicken.
Bring sauce back to a low boil and stir in a little of the cornstarch mixture to lightly thicken it. Spoon the sauce over the chicken. Sprinkle almonds and chives on top. Serve immediately.
